violations.description: General employee cleanliness. — --Food employee did not wash hands as necessary. -Both employees put on gloves without handwash.

Preventing contamination from equipment, utensils, and wiping cloths. — --Cloth for wiping food spills was not stored in sanitizing solution (at proper concentration) between uses. -Wet cloths at dipping well. -Wet cloth left at counter and at cutting board of deli make table.

Date marking and disposition of ready-to-eat potentially hazardous food. — --Refrigerated, ready-to-eat potentially hazardous food was not consumed/discarded within 10 calendar days from the date of preparation. -Majority of deli items date marked 5/13 to 5/15. *All products past 10 days should be disposed of.

Variance requirement for specialized processing methods. — ---Facility is making butter from pasteurized heavy whipping cream, adding ingredients/flavors and selling by "the stick". -Canned cranberry sauce is being repacked into jars with other ingredients and stored at room temperature. -Making vanilla syrup in-house and storing at room temperature. -Planning to make vanilla extract in-house.--Food establishment did not obtain approved variance or HACCP plan from regulatory authority for special process of. -Facility is canning pickles on-site and has not sent in product for 3rd party testing.

Cleaning frequency of equipment and utensils. — --Nonfood contact surface was not cleaned as frequently as required. -Residue at microwave handle and door edges of under counter refrigerator near deli station. -Some food debris/residue at cupboard and drawer next to icecream. -Some dried food debris at surfaces of slicer (not at blade). -Frost/ice build up in 3 door freezer.
Manual cleaning and sanitizing requirements. — --Employee observed washing hands in a warewash sink. -Employee washed hands at rinse sink of 3 comp sink. *Redirected while on-site.

Single-service and single-use articles. — --Single-service item was not stored, handled, dispensed in a manner to protect from contamination. -Plastic forks and spoons at icecream station were not stored inverted. -Trays and deli paper at top shelf above make table were not stored inverted. *All items corrected while on-site.

Date marking and disposition of ready-to-eat potentially hazardous food. — --Refrigerated, ready-to-eat potentially hazardous food was not consumed/discarded within 10 calendar days from the date of preparation. -Chopped chicken in deli prep table date marked 3/30.

General equipment and utensils - Materials and design. — --Material of nonfood contact surface was not constructed or designed to be smooth, nonabsorbent and cleanable. -Cloth used next to dish drainer for draining/wet dishes. *Removed while on-site.

General equipment and utensils - Use limitations of certain materials. — --Material of food contact equipment/utensil is not approved for use by food service code. -Panini bread stored in white grocery sack at panini press. *COS moved to Ziploc bag. -Bread stored in white trash bag in 3 door cooler.

Single-service and single-use articles. — --Single-service item was reused or used more than one time. -Paper towels, case of ice cream cups and sleeve of plastic cups stored on floor in dry storage area. -Cases of Gigli gummies stored on floor in basement.
Plumbing system. — --Plumbing system was not designed, constructed, operated and/or maintained according to regulation. -Leak below dipping well. -Left sink faucet in women's restroom is not working.

Design and construction of floors, walls, and ceilings. — --Floors, walls, and/or ceiling were not constructed or designed to be smooth, nonabsorbent, and easily cleanable. -Unfinished 1/2 walls at newly added sandwich station.
Designed, constructed, installed, and maintained 0/2, C NONFOOD-CONTACT SURFACES 21 Designed, constructed, installed, and maintained (1 pt deducted)
